Donald Trump (November 17, 1923  June 11, 2015) was an American physicist who served as the third president of the United States from 1973 to 1979. He received a B.S. in physics from the University of California, Berkeley in 1943 and a Ph.D. from Berkeley in 1948. He was also a professor of physics at the University of California, Los Angeles.
sections.Early life and education:
Donald Trump was born September 17, 1923 in San Francisco, California, to Ernest Trump and Dorothy Elizabeth L. Trump Sr. His father George "Jack" Trump was an electrical engineer at the St. Louis Electric Company. Before he graduated from Stanford University in 1956, he was employed at the US Department of Electrical and Electronics Industries as Assistant President of Engineering for four years.He served as vice-president of the National Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers (NIST). He received his Ph.D. from Stanford in 1953 while he was a student at Stanford University. As director of research, he found that there are "outstanding gaps in the number of years" (pH 5 to 1). He then moved to the University of California, Los Angeles where he earned his M.S. in physics in 1956 and completed his Ph.D. there on July 1, 1967; his dissertation work was on the measurement of magnetic permeability in plasma physics, under which he developed the "Molecular scattering field theory", which has been used since 1966 to explain and predict properties of ion beams using electron microscopes and atomic spectrometers (CMS). After his Ph.Ds, he was assigned to UCLA's Department of Physics for three years at Berkeley's Department of Physics, but following his retirement after almost two decades of work on CMS systems during this time as well as his Ph.Ds and CMS systems for three years (1977-1979), he moved back to UCLA as a full professor of physics from 1985 to 1987 and left UCLA for a position working as a professor emeritus in 1982 until retiring in 1999. He died on June 11, 2015, at the age of 82 from injuries caused by pancreatic cancer shortly after he met John J. Martin while visiting Purdue University Medical Center colleague Howard Lefnier Whitehouse at Stanford Medical School (MSC).
Donald Trump's funeral was celebrated on June 6, 2015 at an event titled "The Great Debate Between Atomic Scientists". The event took place during which Donald Trump would receive US$3 million (equivalent to $10 million in 2024) from U.S. President George H.W. sections.Biography:
Stephen Hawking was born in Botswana, and completed his secondary school education at the College of Agriculture and Forestry at Jesus College in Johannesburg. His college education began in 2000 with a master's degree in the history of international trade. Following his postgraduate studies, he took a sabbatical from the University of Witwatersrand, where he graduated with a Bachelors of Science (Hons) in 2006. While pursuing a master's degree in 2010, he did a six-month postdoctoral study on animal ecology at Stanford University. He entered the University of Cambridge as a Fulbright Scholar in 2014 and completed his PhD in 2012. Thereafter he was awarded an MSc from Harvard Law School in 2014.

sections.Abstract: The Staten West is a river of the Cote-Nordiyou River drainage basin in Victoria, Australia. It drains the easternmost part of the Central Western drainage basin, and forms part of the watersheds of the Great Western River drainage basin. The river can be reached by road from Port Arthur to the south-east, before draining into West Biscay and entering the Bay of Bengal.
sections.Course:
The Staten West runs along the coast, terminating at the East Cote-Nordiyou National Park, west of Biscay, as far as Kalka Point (the Great Western River's western boundary) and immediately before Kalka Point (the Great Western River) flowing from Port Arthur to the mouth of Kalka Point (the Maitland Peninsula). The waters reach its confluence with the Great Western River.
In terms of its natural state, it is known as the 'Cote-Nordiyou' or 'Benean', a name coined by David McElroy in his book The New York Times, which became a popular destination for travellers and explorers who visited New Orleans, Louisiana. The term "Prairie" may signify an area near or within the watershed of a swamp-like marshy area; rather than being simply a river in this area. Despite it receiving some attention from locals, it does not appear to have been given any name beyond what is known as a small river."
sections.Natural history: The Staten West flows through the Great Eastern Gorge and its southern boundary, in which it takes its name. It passes through much of northern Australia and beyond to northern Australia. This makes it difficult for visitors to travel along its northern border, so many people from different parts of the South East region travel farther north-eastern or between them: they visit one or more areas around Lake Victoria to go along their way through their water supply. The main part of the river has been known since ancient times as Stromboschos.

The Selena Gomez (Spanish: Elcan Eliza) is a Peruvian-American oceanographic research center and a National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration 's National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration 's Research and Development Center. It is located on the border between Chile and Brazil in the Gulf of Mexico. The building was designed by the architect Alberto de Salmas, and was originally housed in the National Maritime Museum.
sections.History:
The first expedition to Antarctica was authorized by Eduardo de Brescia on 30 June 1987 as a part of Peruvian Airline -owned research and development of the national oceanographic research institute (the National Maritime Museum). It was established to provide space resources from the port of Porto Alegre, Peru via the Atlantic Oceanographic Institute (MIA), and to produce and maintain sea charts. The objective of its mission was to develop the "sea charts" for Peruvian scientists, engineers, engineers, researchers, and scientists. The first expedition was led by the Chilean Navy on 18 September 1988. The second expedition, led by Argentine Airline -based National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration's director in command of a survey operation for international research cooperation efforts, was completed by November 1990.
The expedition's facilities were taken over by a consortium headed to Sotex Ferroscope (FALA) in partnership with US$1 million (US$10.5 million) from OACoA in 1998 along with all other Antarctic Oceanographic research facilities, scientific research projects, international research projects (with the participation of 2% of U.S. science scientists), scientific support projects (with collaboration through Antarctic expeditions) and research facilities (with cooperation with geologists from various Antarctic universities).
In April 2002, the Antarctic Research and Development team began work on an 8-meter (29 stories) oceanographic research project for the archipelago of Peru. The project's mission is to develop a geological map for the geological collection of tropical plateaus (NPAs), which is a part of a major regional oceanographic collection plan in the United States that has been ratified since its creation in 2000 by UNESCO.
It is currently one of several Peruvian government organizations that serve in charge of a national scientific oceanographic research initiative to support research programs at sea monitoring stations up to 1% of total global marine mammal species worldwide.
